translated: This isn't exactly about Ena's art, but...
Before this, I also sang the song "I am Rain."
Back when we were doing the story about Ena's art—
I don't really like saying "Ena's drawings."
Anyway, I happened to visit an art museum around then.
I was able to act while reflecting on what it means to look at art, and I got to know Ena better.
And this time, I just happened to be
walking down the street,
and there it was.
It was like the place Ena attends.
A school for people aiming for a career in art
was right there on that road.
I thought, "Oh, so this is what it's like."
Even just looking from the outside,
I felt this intense tension and realized she's working in a very strict environment, which I kept in mind during the recording.
I try to act every day
with the hope of conveying even a little bit of the atmosphere of the world Ena lives in.
Thank you very much.
Alright,
it looks like we still have a few dozen seconds left.
By now, some of you might have already seen the AfterLive.
Since it takes place after the story, you get to see Ena's refreshed face.
Things are going to be tough for her from here on out,
but I hope her expression
and her singing voice,
which show she's truly made up her mind,
are reaching all of you
right at this very moment.
Yeah.
Right?
Man, it's such a good story.
Ena is...
She's so loved, getting a wonderful story like this.
And with that...
